name: Makakilo
desc: Hillside above Kapolei — views, cooler breezes, slightly more upscale than Ewa plains, quiet streets.
insider: The views from upper Makakilo streets — especially along Makakilo Drive near the top — rival the most expensive ridgeline communities on Oahu
It's noticeably windier up here than the Kapolei flats below — great for staying cool but secure patio furniture and anything lightweight
Kapolei Golf Course at the base of the hill is well-maintained and affordable by Oahu standards — perfect for casual golfers
The commute down Makakilo Drive to the H-1 takes about 10 minutes — but the peace and views up top are worth the extra drive
There's no shopping or restaurants in Makakilo itself — you're driving down to Kapolei for everything, even a gallon of milk
The Palehua neighborhood at the very top has the newest homes and best views — also the highest HOA fees
Makakilo Community Park has a nice playground and pavilion, and the annual community fair is a solid family event
Wild goats roam the upper hillside — they're fun to watch but they'll eat any garden that isn't fenced
Homes here start around $800K-$1M for a 3-bedroom — a premium over the Ewa/Kapolei flats but you're paying for the views and breeze
Historically this hillside was part of the Honouliuli ahupuaa — one of the largest land divisions on Oahu, stretching from the mountains to the sea
